log_entries.thought_preview: The sample is a PE32 executable with a size of 367800 bytes, and it has been run in CAPA, Tencent HABO, and VirusTotal Jujubox sandboxes. The tags suggest that it is a spreader with runtime modules an
The sample does not appear to have any network activity, but it does create 2 processes and 0 files. It also creates 7 registry persistence keys and makes a highlighted API call to SetWindowsHookExA.
The sample creates two processes, cmd.exe and reg.exe, and loads several notable modules, including kernel32.dll, msctf.dll, and ole32.dll. It also makes a highlighted API call to SetWindowsHookExA.

The sample creates 7 registry persistence keys, including keys to debug ZhuDongFangYu.exe, 360tray.exe, and taskmgr.exe, and also sets the open command for several file types to a malicious executable
The sample does not appear to drop any files.
The CAPA sandbox has 3 MITRE ATT&CK techniques, 11 signature matches, and no pcap or memdump data.
